title: Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance * Transgender Individuals - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Records/Licenses/Permits Fee Payment Assistance * Transgender Individuals 4 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Transgender Individuals Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH HOOD RIVER CLINIC LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services specifically for the LGBTQ2+ community, including gender-affirming care (initiation and maintenance of hormone therapy and referrals to gender-affirming care specialists), behavioral health providers with specialized training in LGBTQ2+ care, assistance with legal ID changes through the county, DMV, and passports, reproductive health care and STI screening. 849 Pacific Avenue Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Varies by service, established One Community Health patients only Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 8am-3pm On-call provider and nurse: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 386-6380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH THE DALLES CLINIC LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of services specifically for the LGBTQ2+ community, including gender-affirming care (initiation and maintenance of hormone therapy and referrals to gender-affirming care specialists), behavioral health providers with specialized training in LGBTQ2+ care, assistance with legal ID changes through the county, DMV, and passports, reproductive health care and STI screening. 1040 Webber Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Varies by service, established One Community Health patients only Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm, Saturday 8am-3pm On-call provider and nurse: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 296-4610 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN ID PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with changing name/gender through the courts or at the DMV (cannot do both for one person). Services range from providing information on the process of changing documents in the state of Oregon to offering financial aid and advocacy at the courts and the DMV. The level of financial assistance and eligibility for this assistance varies based on program's budget. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: People who identify as transgender or gender-nonconforming Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 535-3828 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSPONDER RESOURCE NAVIGATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral resources including healthcare, insurance, social services, housing, and mental health. Offers an online directory of trans-inclusive local resources, covering everything from healthcare and bodywork to faith services and crisis support. Assists with name/gender legal document change through the courts or at the DMV, with services ranging from providing information about changing documents in Oregon to offering financial aid for document fees. The level of financial assistance and eligibility for this assistance varies based on the program's budget. The message line is staffed by volunteers and it may take a few days for calls to be returned. 440 Maxwell Road Eugene, OR 97404 Eligibility: Individuals who are transgender or gender-diverse Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 321-0872 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE ONE COMMUNITY HEALTH LGBTQ2+ HEALTH CARE OUTSIDE IN ID PROJECT TRANSPONDER RESOURCE NAVIGATOR
